name: Irish Whiskey Soda Bread
recipeInstructions:
-----------------------------------GLAZE-----------------------------------
2 teaspoons Irish Whisky 2 Ilk
BREAD: In a large bowl, combine flour, salt, and baking soda. With a pastry blender or two knives, cut in butter until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs. Stir in raisins or currants (if using). In a separate bowl, combine buttermilk, honey, and whisky. Add all at once to dry ingredients; stir just until no dry spots remain. Turn dough out onto lightly floured surface. Knead lightly for one minute. Too much handling will toughen loaves, while too little will inhibit rising. Divide dough into two halves and shape each half into an 8-inch round. Place in greased 8-inch round cake pans. With a floured knife, cut a cross 1/2 inch deep in each loaf.
GLAZE: In a small bowl, combine whisky and milk. Brush loaves with glaze. Bake in oven set to 350°F (180°C) for 35 to 40 minutes or until loaves sound hollow when tapped on bottoms. Remove from pans; let cool on wire racks. Cut into wedges.
